title: Enforce one-file-one-public-definition import rules
goal: Enforce the design rule that every imported file defines exactly one matching public top-level class or module.
context: Imported files must define exactly one public top-level `class` or `module`.
For class files, snake_case(public class name) must match the file basename.
For module files, module name must match the file basename.
Top-level helper functions, variables, and private classes are disallowed in imported files.
steps: Update runner/import loading to classify entry files versus imported files.
Add checker or runner diagnostics for missing, mismatched, or multiple public definitions.
Reject top-level helper definitions in imported files.
Add tests for valid `user.tya -> class User`, valid `util.tya -> module util`, and invalid mismatch/multiple-definition cases.
done_when: File basename matching is enforced for classes and modules.
Entry-file behavior remains separate from imported-file restrictions.
